buildings.title: Registrar
buildings.description: The main office for enrollment, grades, and official student records. This is where you request transcripts and other academic documents.

buildings.title: OSAS
buildings.description: Focuses on student welfare, development, and discipline. Student organizations also report here for event approvals and accreditations.

buildings.title: Guidance
buildings.description: Provides counseling, career guidance, and psychological support for students. It serves as a safe space for those dealing with personal or academic stress.

buildings.title: CSG
buildings.description: The headquarters for the elected student leaders of the campus. Campus-wide events and student initiatives are planned and organized here.

buildings.title: Nexus
buildings.description: Typically a collaborative space or student hub within the campus. It is a great spot for brainstorming, group meetings, or working on projects.

buildings.title: MIS
buildings.description: This office handles the campus's IT infrastructure and network. They are in charge of maintaining student portals and troubleshooting campus systems.

buildings.title: Studio
buildings.description: A dedicated facility for creative outputs, multimedia production, or broadcasting needs. Students use this space for video recording and audio editing assignments.

buildings.title: OJT
buildings.description: Processes requirements and endorsements for student internships. They also assist in connecting the campus with partner companies.

buildings.title: Department of Computer Studies
buildings.description: The base for CS and IT students taking their major subjects. It houses faculty rooms and consultation areas for programming-related courses.

buildings.title: ICT Building B
buildings.description: A facility filled with computer laboratories and technical lecture rooms. Hands-on database, operating systems, and networking classes are usually held here.

buildings.title: CCAT Cafe
buildings.description: A coffee shop or lounge area on campus for students and faculty. It is a perfect spot for a quick snack while coding or doing assignments.

buildings.title: Comfort Room 3
buildings.description: A public restroom facility designated for student and staff use. It is maintained for the hygiene and convenience of the campus population.

buildings.title: Covered Court 2
buildings.description: An open-air, roofed facility for sports, PE classes, and intramurals. It is also used for large campus gatherings and student assemblies.

buildings.title: Hostel
buildings.description: An accommodation facility for guests, visiting faculty, or sometimes students. It provides temporary lodging right inside the campus.

buildings.title: Department of Management Studies
buildings.description: The home building for business and management programs. Professors and staff for business administration courses have their offices here.

buildings.title: TESDA
buildings.description: An office or training center for Technical Education and Skills Development Authority programs. Vocational courses and national certification assessments are conducted here.

buildings.title: Accreditation Office
buildings.description: In charge of the quality assurance of the school's academic programs. They prepare documents for accrediting bodies to maintain campus standards.

buildings.title: ODI Office
buildings.description: Focuses on academic policies and curriculum management. They monitor teaching quality and oversee faculty development.

buildings.title: Education Department
buildings.description: The base for future teachers regarding their classes and department activities. Requirements for practice teaching are also processed here.

buildings.title: CCAT E-Library
buildings.description: A modern library facility focused more on digital resources and computer access. It is a great place for quiet online research when you need an internet connection.

buildings.title: Canteen
buildings.description: The main dining area of the campus where meals and snacks are sold. Friends usually meet here during lunch breaks or free periods.

buildings.title: EBA
buildings.description: This is the official school supply of the campus. Where students buy their miscellaneous stuff.

buildings.title: Mechanical Department
buildings.description: The main facility for mechanical engineering or technology students. It houses heavy equipment and workshops for hands-on technical training.

buildings.title: Clinic
buildings.description: The campus health center for medical and dental emergencies. You can get first aid and basic health consultations here.

buildings.title: Automotive Department
buildings.description: A dedicated building for automotive technology students. Practical exams and training for vehicle maintenance and repair take place here.

buildings.title: Research Extension
buildings.description: Handles institutional research and community extension projects. They facilitate seminars and the school's community outreach programs.

buildings.title: Accounting Office
buildings.description: Processes tuition fees, student accounts, and other financial transactions. They also issue clearance for balances and examination permits.

buildings.title: Department of Engineering
buildings.description: The main hub for engineering students and faculty. Major technical classes and heavy laboratory experiments are held here.

buildings.title: Supply Office
buildings.description: In charge of the procurement and distribution of school properties and equipment. Departments get their office supplies and conduct inventory here.

buildings.title: Cashier
buildings.description: The official payment center of the campus for enrollment and miscellaneous fees. Allowances or refunds can also be claimed here.

buildings.title: Office of Campus Administrator
buildings.description: The main executive office for the head of the campus. Major administrative decisions and campus management planning are done here.

buildings.title: Soon
buildings.description: A placeholder label for a facility or building that is still under construction or in the planning stage. Its official purpose will be revealed once finished.

buildings.title: SHS Building
buildings.description: The dedicated building for Senior High School students. All their classrooms and faculty offices are located here.

buildings.title: PPSS
buildings.description: In charge of campus maintenance, janitorial services, and safety. They maintain the buildings and ensure the security of the entire school.

buildings.title: Covered Court 1
buildings.description: The primary indoor sports and events facility of the campus. Large ceremonies, graduations, and major sporting events are usually held here.

buildings.title: Comfort Room 2
buildings.description: Another designated public restroom area for the convenience of students and campus visitors.

buildings.title: Pavillion 2
buildings.description: Another shaded structure space on campus that is perfect for group studies or taking a break outdoors.

buildings.title: Pavillion 2
buildings.description: Another shaded structure space on campus that is perfect for group studies or taking a break outdoors.

buildings.title: Pavillion 1
buildings.description: A shaded structure used for outdoor classes, often used for minor school gatherings or student socialization.

buildings.title: Comfort Room 1
buildings.description: A standard restroom facility situated within a key area of the campus for public use.

buildings.title: Technovation Building
buildings.description: A modern facility likely dedicated to innovation, research, and advanced tech projects. It may contain specialized laboratories or startup incubation spaces.

buildings.title: ICT Building A
buildings.description: The counterpart to Building B, also focused on computer and information technology operations. It is the current faculty of DCS instructors

buildings.title: Administration Building 1
buildings.description: Houses the key administrative offices of the campus. Top officials, human resources, and central operations are usually based here.

buildings.title: Administration Building 2
buildings.description: An extension of the main admin building for other administrative services and support staff.

buildings.title: Building 2
buildings.description: A general academic building containing standard classrooms for various minor and major subjects.

buildings.title: Building 1
buildings.description: Another primary academic structure used for general education classes, lectures, and block sections.

buildings.title: Department of Arts and Sciences
buildings.description: The base for general education subjects and arts/science majors. Professors for math, English, sciences, and humanities are stationed here.

buildings.title: Library
buildings.description: The main traditional library of the campus housing printed books, journals, and thesis archives.
